task_text: Put together an agenda for next week's Q3 kickoff meeting. The meeting is 90 minutes and should cover the most important topics for the team to align on before Q3 starts. Save it as q3_kickoff_agenda.txt.

content.text: Let me start by checking your working directory for any existing files that might contain relevant context (notes, goals, previous agendas, etc.) before putting together the agenda.

content.content: ERROR: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: ''

content.text: No existing files to reference, so I'll craft a comprehensive, well-structured 90-minute Q3 kickoff agenda from scratch and save it now.

input.content:
================================================================
                    Q3 KICKOFF MEETING AGENDA
================================================================
Duration:  90 Minutes
Purpose:   Align the team on priorities, goals, and execution
           plan heading into Q3.
----------------------------------------------------------------

AGENDA AT A GLANCE
------------------
 1. Welcome & Setting the Stage          (5 min)
 2. Q2 Retrospective – Wins & Learnings  (15 min)
 3. Q3 Vision & Strategic Priorities     (15 min)
 4. OKRs / Goals Breakdown by Team       (20 min)
 5. Key Initiatives & Roadmap Walkthrough(15 min)
 6. Cross-Team Dependencies & Risks      (10 min)
 7. Ways of Working & Norms for Q3       (5 min)
 8. Open Q&A & Closing Remarks           (5 min)
----------------------------------------------------------------

DETAILED BREAKDOWN
------------------

[00:00 – 00:05]  1. WELCOME & SETTING THE STAGE (5 min)
   Owner: Meeting Facilitator / Team Lead
   • Welcome attendees and confirm agenda
   • Quickly state the purpose: why this meeting matters
   • Establish meeting norms (cameras on, questions at the end
     of each section, parking lot for off-topic items)

[00:05 – 00:20]  2. Q2 RETROSPECTIVE – WINS & LEARNINGS (15 min)
   Owner: Team Lead / Department Heads
   • Celebrate key Q2 wins and milestones hit
   • Review metrics: what we hit, what we missed, and why
   • Highlight top 2–3 lessons learned to carry into Q3
   • Keep it forward-focused — what will we do differently?

[00:20 – 00:35]  3. Q3 VISION & STRATEGIC PRIORITIES (15 min)
   Owner: Senior Leadership
   • Restate the company/team mission and how Q3 fits in
   • Share the 3–5 strategic priorities for the quarter
   • Connect the "why" behind each priority
   • Explain how success will be measured at the quarter's end

[00:35 – 00:55]  4. OKRs / GOALS BREAKDOWN BY TEAM (20 min)
   Owner: Team Leads (each takes 3–4 min)
   • Each team/function presents their Q3 OKRs or KPIs
   • Call out the single most important metric to move
   • Flag any goal that depends on another team's output
   • Quick gut-check: does everyone feel these are achievable
     yet ambitious?

[00:55 – 01:10]  5. KEY INITIATIVES & ROADMAP WALKTHROUGH (15 min)
   Owner: Project Leads / Product / Operations
   • Walk through the major initiatives and projects for Q3
   • Highlight key milestones and target dates
   • Note any big launches, campaigns, or deliverables
   • Identify resource or capacity considerations

[01:10 – 01:20]  6. CROSS-TEAM DEPENDENCIES & RISKS (10 min)
   Owner: Facilitator + All Teams
   • Surface known blockers or dependencies between teams
   • Discuss top 2–3 risks and mitigation strategies
   • Confirm owners for shared workstreams
   • Decide what goes into the parking lot for follow-up

[01:20 – 01:25]  7. WAYS OF WORKING & NORMS FOR Q3 (5 min)
   Owner: Team Lead / Operations
   • Confirm cadence: standups, check-ins, reviews
   • Reaffirm communication channels and escalation paths
   • Highlight any process changes taking effect in Q3
   • Remind team of key recurring dates (all-hands, retros,
     planning sessions)

[01:25 – 01:30]  8. OPEN Q&A & CLOSING REMARKS (5 min)
   Owner: Facilitator
   • Open the floor for final questions or clarifications
   • Summarize the top 3 takeaways from the meeting
   • Call out immediate next steps and who owns them
   • End on an energizing note — set the tone for a great Q3!

----------------------------------------------------------------

POST-MEETING ACTION ITEMS
--------------------------
   □ Distribute meeting notes & OKR summary to all attendees
   □ Log cross-team dependencies in the project tracker
   □ Schedule any follow-up sessions flagged in the parking lot
   □ Confirm Q3 recurring meeting cadence on team calendars

================================================================
              Let's make Q3 our best quarter yet!
================================================================

content.text:
The agenda has been saved as **q3_kickoff_agenda.txt**! Here's a summary of how the 90 minutes are structured:

| # | Section | Time | Duration |
|---|---------|------|----------|
| 1 | Welcome & Setting the Stage | 0:00 | 5 min |
| 2 | Q2 Retrospective – Wins & Learnings | 0:05 | 15 min |
| 3 | Q3 Vision & Strategic Priorities | 0:20 | 15 min |
| 4 | OKRs / Goals Breakdown by Team | 0:35 | 20 min |
| 5 | Key Initiatives & Roadmap Walkthrough | 0:55 | 15 min |
| 6 | Cross-Team Dependencies & Risks | 1:10 | 10 min |
| 7 | Ways of Working & Norms for Q3 | 1:20 | 5 min |
| 8 | Open Q&A & Closing Remarks | 1:25 | 5 min |

**A few design decisions worth noting:**
- **Q2 retro comes first** — grounding the team in what just happened makes the Q3 goals feel more meaningful and connected.
- **Vision before OKRs** — leadership sets the "why" before teams dive into the "what," so everyone has the same context.
- **Dependencies & risks get dedicated time** — this is often skipped but is critical for avoiding mid-quarter surprises.
- **Post-meeting action items** are baked in so nothing falls through the cracks after the room clears.

Let me know if you'd like to adjust any section, add specific agenda items, or create a calendar invite for the meeting!
